note: Tracked canonical-JSON ledger; a deliberate human/agent ritual, NEVER a runner post-step (mirrors graduation-ledger.json). Counts are COMPUTED from entries, never narrated. Refuse-on-unreadable: a present-but-corrupt ledger is a hard error, never reset to empty. The both-directions check_repo_contracts backstop is IMPLEMENTED (tools/check_repo_contracts.py miss_postmortem_issues, folded into run; incompleteness advisories on stderr): every entry's mechanism_added MUST resolve to a landed artifact (each named repo path exists; each named art-anchor id is in the registry) and every executed plant's revert_scope sha256 pins MUST still match the tree — the pins are recorded at the byte-identical revert, so the check is position-independent (it holds mid-slice as well as post-commit, where a live git status would false-red on unrelated in-flight edits). Entry #1's plant EXECUTED 2026-07-22 (windowed, Godot 4.6.1-stable, 1152x648, forward_plus); the run artifacts are machine-local (.godot-smoke is gitignored: proof-class1-compare.json first-break, proof-class1-update-refusal.json regen-under-break RED, proof-class1-revert-compare.json post-revert GREEN, proof-vlm-plugin-degrade.json opt-in reviewer degrade) — the DURABLE proof is the revert_scope pins below plus the backstop that holds them. Entry #2's plant (the nav_audit false-red; the protocol's first FALSE-RED variant) EXECUTED 2026-07-22 (headless, PLAYTEST_FORCE_HEADLESS, Godot 4.6.1-stable): a forced turn-1 trap stayed GREEN and stream-identical across 20 fresh subprocesses under the seed while removing the seed diverged 10/10 streams and the pre-fix RUN check went 3/3 RED; the pre-fix harness reproduced the reasonless red that the fixed harness reports with reasons — the durable proof is the revert_scope pins below (the FIXED-tree bytes, since the mechanism landed before the plant ran). Entry #2's scripts/app/nav_audit.gd pin was RE-STAMPED 2026-07-22 after a GATED_SCAN_RADIUS hardening (20->40): a live world measured its nearest surf gate on ring 22 from a real saved player tile, so the tighter bound made the pass event hostage to the save's geometry (deterministic RED after any mid-run save drift, e.g. a harness kill — the save-geometry sibling this entry's classes_silent already discloses); the gate CONTRACTS the entry's mechanism proved are unchanged (same surf/cut proofs, wider scan). The pin follows the fixed tree through legitimate code change — the documented maintenance route, like fixing a broken internal link.

instance.description: Programmatic HP bars (EnemyHPBar/PlayerHPBar ColorRects in scenes/ui/BattleView.tscn) were misaligned +11 stage-px from the baked GSC tracks: enemy at x43 (track x32), player at x107 (track x96). The committed baseline sidecar (fd7d0925e0aed24c9cb0f04526a3368717dfac72, 2026-07-18) froze the wrong draw_order EnemyHPBar rect [43,18,48,4] and the suite stayed GREEN against it for 3+ days. Every automated check class was self-referential: truth was the code's own output or a human transcription of it, so nothing could see an overlay sitting off the art feature it belongs on.
instance.perturbation: Fix: EnemyHPBar offset_left 43->32 and PlayerHPBar offset_left 107->96 (-11 stage px each), then visual_sweep_update regenerated exactly the four battle baselines 09-12 (overworld/menu baselines byte-identical).

classes_silent.why_silent: The wrong render WAS the baseline; delta 0.
No clusters vs its own baseline; the bar moved WITH the baseline.
draw_order delta checks z/y_sort/presence only, never rects; baseline and fresh sidecars both carried the code's [43,18,...].
HP bars were never modeled — no bar rect in the ACTION_*/MOVE_*/ITEM_* constants.
HP numbers are not ANCHOR strings; the glyph oracle matches anchor-mode strings only.
Checks name/level overlap only; consumes the layout model, not art.
Label rects unchanged; a translation changes no contrast ratio.
Self-consistent scale replication at every window size; the offset replicates cleanly.

classes_silent.class: rubric line 63 'Are HP bars on their baked tracks...?'
classes_silent.why_silent: Asked by the rubric, answered by NO agent — the model-reviewer lane was designed but never populated.

entries.mechanism_added: SA anchor class: docs/registry/art-anchors.toml entries battle/enemy_hp_track + battle/player_hp_track (dark-run stage_rects (32,18,48,4)/(96,74,48,4) re-derived from battle_screen2.png bytes); tools/art_geometry.py derive_rect; tools/check_art_anchors.py art_anchor_issues folded into check_repo_contracts.run (RED static gate); anchor_drift class in tools/vision_review.py default_reviewer (quarantine-tier, art:<id> regions); tools/run_playtests.py apply_anchor_gate baseline-regeneration refusal — active for BOTH visual_sweep and visual_sweep_update via VISUAL_SWEEP_SCENARIOS (a guard on the compare name alone leaves the refusal branch unreachable, which the plant's first pass exposed and fixed), plus the VISION_REVIEWER_CMD opt-in wiring of the --reviewer-cmd socket into apply_vision_review; EA answerer: tools/vlm_reviewer.py (reviewer_kind model-qwen3-vl, composite degrade, kinds_ran contract) populating the socket for the battle 'hp bars on their baked tracks' question — opt-in in the runner (default unset ⇒ deterministic lane), the first model-answered manifest pending a qwen3-vl pull; protocol backstop: tools/check_repo_contracts.py miss_postmortem_issues (both-directions, folded into run) resolving this mechanism_added to landed artifacts and holding this plant's revert_scope. All resolve to landed artifacts in this slice.

plant.perturbation: CLASS-LEVEL: re-break EnemyHPBar/PlayerHPBar offsets to 43/107 WITHOUT regenerating baselines (any overlay off its art anchor), run every lane; then ALSO regenerate baselines under the break (--scenario visual_sweep_update) and re-run. Both bars are broken; only the enemy anchor can fire live-respect today — PlayerHUD/PlayerHPBar is nested and not yet exposed in the sidecar draw_order (recursive collection pending), so the player anchor is recorded UNVERIFIABLE, never silently passed.
plant.revert_proof: git diff empty over the plant scope (byte-identical revert; the eight battle baseline files are sha256-pinned by revert_scope and mechanically held by the check_repo_contracts miss_postmortem_issues backstop). EXECUTED 2026-07-22 (windowed, Godot 4.6.1-stable official, 1152x648, forward_plus, HEAD cf44597). FIRST BREAK (EnemyHPBar 32->43 + PlayerHPBar 96->107, no baseline regen, compare mode — machine-local .godot-smoke/proof-class1-compare.json): every coded RED tier stayed silent (region_failures=0; global backstop 0.28% < 0.5%; canary/ink/string/label/glyph/contrast/CVD/display-matrix all zero) while anchor_drift fired [quarantine] on the moved enemy bar — and two enumerated classes independently CORROBORATED at the QUARANTINE tier, grounded on the moved bar (visual_region_diff region_drift: 10 'unexplained' clusters; Lane-4 deterministic sidecar-consistency cluster_unexplained: 10 findings). The silence enumeration is therefore scoped to the BASELINE-FROZEN escape (RED tiers); first-break detection was never the hole — regenerated baselines were. REGEN-UNDER-BREAK (update mode — machine-local .godot-smoke/proof-class1-update-refusal.json): apply_anchor_gate REFUSED the regeneration on all four battle shots (anchor_refusals: battle/enemy_hp_track live [43,18,37,4] vs art [32,18,48,4], tol 1px), RESTORED all 48 prior baseline files byte-identical, and failed RED (summary.ok=false); battle/player_hp_track recorded UNVERIFIABLE (nested node not in draw_order). REVERT: all scope files sha-verified equal to their pre-plant bytes and the post-revert compare run is GREEN (machine-local .godot-smoke/proof-class1-revert-compare.json). NOTE: the plant's first pass exposed and fixed a guard gap — prepare_anchor_gate/apply_anchor_gate keyed on 'visual_sweep' alone made the refusal branch unreachable under 'visual_sweep_update' (VISUAL_SWEEP_SCENARIOS now covers both names; the first unguarded update pass is the proof of that hole).

instance.description: FALSE-RED variant (the suite intermittently LIES; not a miss). nav_audit intermittently failed on FRESH verify_all runs with 'missing required events: nav_audit_passed' — the scenario ran to completion in ~2.6s but never emitted its pass event. The pass event was gated on the outcome of a wild battle whose inputs were drawn from UNSEEDED, wall-clock-seeded RNG (game_runtime.gd and battle_runtime.gd both call _rng.randomize() once per fresh subprocess): when the drawn wild landed an EFFECT_TRAP_TARGET move, the game CORRECTLY refused to flee ('Can't escape!' while is_trapped, view stays visible) and _check_run_escape recorded that legitimate mechanic as a suite failure, suppressing nav_audit_passed. Compounding the red into a REASONLESS one: the failure reason was emitted only via push_error ('ERROR: Nav audit failed: ...'), and ERROR_MARKERS=('SCRIPT ERROR','Parse Error') never matched the 'ERROR: ' prefix, so exceptions:[] and the report showed only the blank missing-event. Investigations exonerated the create_timer/cold-start hypothesis (nav_audit uses only tile_changed/process_frame awaits; failing runs last ~2.6s against a 120s budget): the wall-clock dependence was in the RNG SEED — which is why cold-start only correlated (it shifts the randomize() seed), why the true failure rate was ~3-5% regardless of temperature, why re-runs passed (new seed), why 10/10 greens occurred (luck), and why a git-stash test proved it PRE-EXISTING at clean HEAD cf44597 (the unseeded draw and the unversioned user:// save both live outside the working tree).
shots: nav_audit (fresh headless subprocess runs under verify_all / run_playtests.py --include-smoke)
instance.perturbation: Fix (instance closed AS A SIDE EFFECT of the class mechanism): pin both runtime rngs via seed_for_smoke(NAV_AUDIT_SEED := 20260718) before _start_battle (the house seeding convention), and make _check_run_escape total — a visible view after RUN must carry the game's ONLY refusal text 'Can't escape!' (proving the trap-refusal mechanic as NEW coverage), then the trap is deterministically expired (battle_runtime._player_mon['trap_turns'] = 0) and RUN re-proven to end the battle. A legitimate in-game escape refusal can never again be recorded as a suite failure; the traversal/menu halves are untouched (no battle rng), and the save backup/restore guard is untouched.

classes_silent.why_silent: Checks only that nav_audit_passed is present; the scenario RAN and simply never emitted it, so the check reported 'missing' correctly but with no reason — there was no failure-event contract symmetric with the _passed special-casing.

('SCRIPT ERROR','Parse Error') does not match push_error's 'ERROR: <message>' prefix (the 'at:' backtrace line carries no marker either), so the emitted failure reason was discarded into exceptions:[] — the reason existed and was thrown away.
Pins the visual_sweep CAPTURE determinism (project.godot msaa/snap pins, canary shot hash); never covers scenario PASS EVENTS or the rng draws feeding them — a different determinism surface.
Proves the soak's OWN bot rng deterministic; nav_audit never adopted the convention — its wild draw, enemy move choice, and trap/attract rolls rode the per-process randomize().

Seeds the battle rng for a HAND-CRAFTED wild (visual_sweep never drives generate_wild_encounter); no seam covered the real encounter-draw path nav_audit exercises, and nothing pinned the game-side _rng at all.
Track landed mechanisms and graduation streaks, not run-to-run determinism of scenario inputs; a flake that re-passes leaves no ledger trace.

Restores the SAVE around each run but cannot pin in-process rng draws (and, as recorded here, cannot protect save geometry from a harness SIGKILL mid-scenario — the latent sibling subclass, advisory, not this entry's fix).

entries.mechanism_added: Two coordinated class-level mechanisms (the instance is fixed as a side effect; neither changes what nav_audit verifies). (A) DETERMINISM — scripts/runtime/game_runtime.gd seed_for_smoke(seed) seam pins BOTH _rng and battle_runtime._rng (mirrors the existing direct-assignment convention at scripts/app/visual_sweep.gd BATTLE_RNG_SEED and scripts/app/display_matrix.gd but covers the whole generate_wild_encounter path), called from scripts/app/nav_audit_battle.gd run() with scripts/app/nav_audit.gd NAV_AUDIT_SEED := 20260718 before _start_battle, so the audited inputs are a pure function of (code, save, seed), reproducible across subprocesses and across cold and warm starts; plus the total RUN contract in scripts/app/nav_audit_battle.gd _check_run_escape — a legitimate 'Can't escape!' trap refusal is PROVEN, the trap expired deterministically, and escape re-proven (strengthened coverage, never relaxed). (C) HONEST REPORTING — shared-harness class fix so EVERY scenario's red names its cause: tools/godot_dap_smoketest.py ERROR_MARKERS widened to capture push_error's 'ERROR: ' prefix and single-sourced (imported by tools/run_playtests.py so both transports agree by construction), plus failed_event_entry: any trace event ending in '_failed' is collected as a named failure with its reasons payload and both finalize and result_summary mark not-ok and record it; scripts/app/nav_audit.gd emits nav_audit_failed with the failures array on the non-pass branch alongside push_error (the going-forward contract for every audit's non-pass branch), documented in docs/references/trace-events.md and held by tools/check_repo_contracts.py (nav_audit_failed in app_bootstrap required_trace_events; the miss_postmortem_issues backstop holds this entry's revert_scope pins). Handled engine-side refusals stay quiet at the source — scripts/runtime/save_store.gd parses corrupt saves via JSON.new().parse (error code, no engine output) — so the widened capture adds no false reds on green paths (proven by a full headless suite run). The class — unseeded pass-events plus silent non-pass exits suite-wide — is closed by (A) as the convention every scenario adopts plus (C) in the shared harness. All resolve to landed artifacts in this slice.

plant.perturbation: CLASS-LEVEL, two parts, headless (PLAYTEST_FORCE_HEADLESS), then byte-identical revert. PART 1 (determinism backstop): force the instance's KIND — the wild's moveset replaced by a single perfect-accuracy EFFECT_TRAP_TARGET move with unkillable HP in _start_battle, so turn 1 ALWAYS applies the partial trap to the player through the real mechanic (choose_enemy_move_index -> execute_attack -> apply_trap). P1-1: mechanism in place, 20 fresh subprocesses (warm + mtime-cold + one .godot-move true-cold) -> 20/20 GREEN with the trap refusal PROVEN (battle_options_checked 11 vs the untrapped 10) and the ts_msec-normalized event stream byte-identical across the 19 same-temperature runs (the one true-cold run forms its OWN identical cold stream; the sole cold/warm delta is the engine cold-cache 'sound' flag on attack_animation_played — not the pinned rng stream, not the pass payload, which is identical). P1-2: seed_for_smoke call removed (unseeded randomize() re-introduced), same forced trap, 10 fresh runs -> all still GREEN (the total RUN contract alone tolerates the trap) but 10/10 DIVERGENT event streams (a different wild species/level every run: SLAKOTH/KAKUNA/QUILLADIN/...) — the non-determinism returns and the class is caught loudly, isolating the seed as the determinism pin. P1-3: seed restored, pre-fix _check_run_escape restored, forced trap -> 3/3 RED with the original instance's exact failure text 'battle: RUN did not end the battle' — the total contract is what turns the trap red into proven behavior. PART 2 (honest-reporting backstop): P2-1 (specimen): nav_audit_failed emission removed, ERROR_MARKERS narrowed to ('SCRIPT ERROR','Parse Error'), pre-fix check + forced trap, run through run_playtests.py -> reproduces the original REASONLESS red byte-for-byte in shape (missing_all=['nav_audit_passed'], exceptions=[], failed_events=[]). P2-2: (C) restored (emission + widened markers), same forced failure -> the report carries its reason on BOTH channels (failed_events=[nav_audit_failed {'failures': ['battle: RUN did not end the battle']}] AND exceptions=['ERROR: Nav audit failed: battle: RUN did not end the battle']) — a red can no longer be reasonless. All perturbations reverted byte-identical (verified by sha256 against the pre-plant snapshots); because the mechanism fix LANDED before the plant executed, the recorded pins are the FIXED-tree bytes, held mechanically by the miss_postmortem_issues backstop from now on.
plant.revert_proof: git-tracked plant scope sha-verified equal to the pre-plant fixed-tree snapshots (byte-identical revert); the pins below are held by the check_repo_contracts miss_postmortem_issues backstop (position-independent: holds mid-slice/uncommitted exactly as post-commit). Plant artifacts (.godot-smoke/plant-p2-1.json reasonless-red specimen, plant-p2-2.json reason-carrying report) are machine-local.
